sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Mylan Stock (MYL) After Hours Today, Dec. 24, 2025: Why Shares Rose in the Christmas Eve Session and What to Watch Before the Next Market Open
feed.summary: Mylan N.V. (NYSE: MYL) shares saw modest gains during an early-close Christmas Eve session and maintained flat performance in after-hours trading. Despite low trading volume, PFE's stock performance reflects investor attention on the hemophilia drug Hympavzi's safety, the company's 2026 outlook, and strategies to address the patent cliff through oncology and obesity pipelines. Key factors influencing future trading include updates on Hympavzi, pipeline developments, policy shifts impacting drug pricing, and anticipated Q4 and full-year 2025 results on February 3, 2026.

feed.title: Cisco Stock (CSCO) After Hours on Dec. 24, 2025: Holiday Close, Today’s News & Forecasts, and What to Watch Before Markets Reopen
feed.summary: On December 24, 2025, Cisco stock (CSCO) closed essentially flat and saw a modest increase in thin after-hours trading due to the early Christmas Eve market close. Key takeaways from the day included an AI-driven networking narrative, new February 2026 options listings, and a slight price target trim from President Capital. Investors should monitor liquidity, cybersecurity updates, and the AI infrastructure narrative when markets reopen on December 26.

feed.title: FirstEnergy notifies PSC of plan to apply for approval of gas-fired power plant project
feed.summary: FirstEnergy Corp. subsidiaries Mon Power and Potomac Edison have notified the Public Service Commission of their intent to file an application for a new combined cycle gas plant. The application will include related rate recovery, and the companies are exploring two paths for building the 1,200 megawatt plant, which is expected to go online in 2028. This move comes as their coal-fired plants, Harrison and Fort Martin, near retirement age, although FirstEnergy plans to keep them operational until 2035.

feed.title: Lawsuit: Ex-Brown & Brown execs hatched holiday plot to poach 200 workers
feed.summary: Brown & Brown Inc. has filed a lawsuit alleging that 29 former top employees conspired with British-based insurer Howden U.S. Services LLC to orchestrate a "mass employee raid," targeting 200 of its workers across multiple states. The lawsuit claims this move was strategically timed over the holidays "to inflict maximum competitive harm" and accuses the defendants of trade secret theft, contractual breaches, and unfair competition. This is not the first time Howden U.S. Services has been accused of poaching workers, with at least three other insurers filing similar lawsuits.

feed.title: Emerson Electric (EMR): Valuation Check as Cold Chain IoT Demand and Automation Tailwinds Lift Investor Optimism

feed.title: Here are the 5 most dramatic CEO exits this year
feed.summary: The year 2025 saw several high-profile CEO exits across major companies like Kohl's, Kroger, Market Basket, Lululemon, and Nestlé. These departures were often due to undisclosed romantic relationships, violations of ethics policies, family feuds, or disagreements over company performance. The article details the specific circumstances surrounding each dramatic CEO departure, highlighting a tumultuous year for C-suite executives.

feed.title: How Investors Are Reacting To FactSet (FDS) AI Data Push and Expanded Share Repurchase Plan
feed.summary: FactSet Research Systems Inc. recently reported strong first-quarter fiscal 2026 results, reaffirmed full-year guidance, and expanded its share repurchase authorization to US$1.00 billion. The company also introduced a production-grade Model Context Protocol server, aiming to integrate its financial data more deeply into clients' AI workflows. This move signals FactSet's strategy to leverage AI for future growth, although ongoing cost rationalization in the asset management and banking sectors remains a key consideration for investors.
